key_insight: DrugGen-2 is a disease-aware generative model that combines disease ontology and protein sequence context to design small molecules for drug discovery.
clinical_relevance: By incorporating disease context, it may improve early-stage hit generation and drug repurposing for disease-specific targets, but it remains a preclinical computational tool.
method: Fine-tuned a pretrained GPT-2 model on approved drug–disease–target data.
Used supervised fine-tuning followed by GRPO reinforcement learning.
Optimized for chemical validity, novelty, diversity, and predicted binding affinity.
evidence_signal: In evaluation on five diabetic nephropathy–relevant protein targets, DrugGen-2 outperformed DrugGPT and DrugGen and produced stronger predicted affinities in docking, including scores better than enalapril at ACE.
watch_item: Findings are based on computational prediction and docking, so real-world efficacy, safety, and synthesis feasibility still need experimental validation.
paper_title: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery
